data.title: As the ticket system, I reject applying brainstorm:complete without evidence of scrutiny
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a PIL-heading guard that rejects `.claude/scripts/dso ticket tag <id> brainstorm:complete` when no `### Planning Intelligence Log` heading exists in the ticket's event log. All other tags bypass the check.

**Why**: `brainstorm:complete` filters `/dso:sprint` epic eligibility. Without a structural guard, any skill or agent can apply it regardless of whether the scrutiny pipeline ran, producing false-positive sprint eligibility. The PIL heading is the load-bearing marker that already appears in brainstorm Phase 3's description template, so reusing it costs no new tag vocabulary.

**Scope**:
- IN:
  - Port `_ticket_has_pil` from `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-migrate-brainstorm-tags.sh` into `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib.sh` (the migration script keeps its own private copy until Story 3 decides the migration-provenance question)
  - Fix `_ticket_has_pil` to correctly scan across all three event shapes: CREATE (`data.description`), EDIT (`data.fields.description`), COMMENT (`data.body`). The existing migration-script copy only reads `data.description` and `data.body`, silently missing any PIL section that arrives via a later EDIT event — which is the production path brainstorm actually uses.
  - Add `_tag_add_checked` helper in `ticket-lib.sh` that wraps `_tag_add` (from Story 1) and rejects `brainstorm:complete` when no PIL heading is detected across the event log. For any other tag, `_tag_add_checked` fast-paths straight to `_tag_add`.
  - `ticket-tag.sh` dispatches through `_tag_add_checked` (not `_tag_add` directly) so the guard is always on the hot path
  - Error message on rejection names the missing `### Planning Intelligence Log` heading and points the caller to `/dso:brainstorm`
- OUT:
  - Guards on any tag other than `brainstorm:complete` — SC3 is explicit
  - Source enforcement ("only skill X may write tag Y") — known system limitation

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, attempting `.claude/scripts/dso ticket tag <id> brainstorm:complete` on a ticket with no `### Planning Intelligence Log` heading in any of its events fails with a non-zero exit and a message naming the missing heading and pointing the caller to `/dso:brainstorm`.
  ← Satisfies: "`ticket tag <id> brainstorm:complete` is rejected with a clear, actionable error when no \"### Planning Intelligence Log\" heading exists in the ticket description or any event (CREATE, EDIT, COMMENT)."
- When this story is complete, `_ticket_has_pil` correctly detects the PIL heading whether it appears in a CREATE event's `data.description`, an EDIT event's `data.fields.description`, or a COMMENT event's `data.body`, with a unit test covering each branch.
  ← Satisfies: "`ticket tag <id> brainstorm:complete` is rejected with a clear, actionable error when no \"### Planning Intelligence Log\" heading exists in the ticket description or any event (CREATE, EDIT, COMMENT)."
- When this story is complete, adding any tag other than `brainstorm:complete` does not trigger a PIL check.
  ← Satisfies: "All other tags add without a PIL check."
- When this story is complete, a round-trip — apply `brainstorm:complete` with PIL present, untag it, then re-apply with PIL still present — succeeds with no lost sibling tags, verified by a unit test.
  ← Satisfies: "A practitioner can complete a round-trip workflow — apply brainstorm:complete, later untag it to re-enter brainstorming, and re-apply it with PIL still present — without any lost tags, errors, or read-merge-write collisions."
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic.

## Considerations

- [Naming] `_tag_add` (Story 1, unguarded) and `_tag_add_checked` (this story, PIL-aware wrapper) are separate functions. The wrapper calls the base. This keeps Story 1's helper name honest at its merge boundary and avoids the interim state where a function named `_tag_add_checked` performs no check.
- [Testing] Acceptance branches to cover: PIL in CREATE description, PIL in EDIT `data.fields.description`, PIL in COMMENT body. Rejection branches: no PIL anywhere in the event log.
- [Maintainability] The migration script `ticket-migrate-brainstorm-tags.sh` keeps its own private `_ticket_has_pil` copy until Story 3 decides the migration-provenance question (option a: keep migration's direct-write path out of the refactor to preserve its explicit author + env_id).

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
